content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with all atom labels visible. Let me locate atom 27 (O:27) in this image.

From the image, I can see O:27 is in the lower right portion of the molecule, colored red (oxygen). Looking at the connections to O:27, I can clearly see it's bonded to:
- C:26 (carbon, yellow/orange)
- C:21 (carbon, yellow/orange)

The bonds are visible as the connecting lines between these atoms. Atom 27 appears to be part of a carbonyl group or similar functional group, connected to two carbon atoms.

Let me verify if there are any other neighbors by examining the structure more carefully. From the image, O:27 has exactly two covalent bonds - one to C:26 and one to C:21.

Regarding the geomconf_index, based on the file name "geomconf_3321.xyz", the geomconf_index appears to be 3321.

So the neighbor atoms of atom 27 (O) are:
- Atom 26: C (carbon)
- Atom 21: C (carbon)

Geomconf_index: 3321

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 27, whose element should be O. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
